verses.text: Nor ought we to fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “If God be for us, who can be against us?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And our enemies the sea hath covered, He hath effaced them in baptism by the remission of sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the next place there followeth, “And He led them into the mountain of His sanctification” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How much better into Holy Church!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The mountain which His right hand hath gotten.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How much higher is the Church which Christ hath gotten, concerning whom has been said, “And to whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He cast forth from the face of them the nations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And from the face of His faithful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For nations in a manner are the evil spirits of Gentile errors.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And by lot He divided unto them the land in the cord of distribution.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in us “all things one and the same Spirit doth work, dividing severally to every one as He willeth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He made to dwell in their tabernacles the tribes of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the tabernacles, he saith, of the Gentiles He made the tribes of Israel to dwell, which I think can better be explained spiritually, inasmuch as unto celestial glory, whence sinning angels have been cast forth and cast down, by Christ’s grace we are being uplifted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that generation crooked and embittering, inasmuch as for these corporal blessings they put not off the coat of oldness, “Did tempt” yet, “and provoked the high God, and His testimonies they kept not (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 56): and they turned them away, and they kept not the covenant, like their fathers” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For under a sort of covenant and decree they said, “All things which our Lord God hath spoken we will do, and we will hear.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a remarkable thing indeed which he saith, “like their fathers:” while throughout the whole text of the Psalm he was seeming to speak of the same men as it were, yet now it appeareth that the words did concern those who were already in the land of promise, and that the fathers spoken of were of those who did provoke in the desert.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They were turned,” he saith, “into a crooked,” or, as some copies have it, “into a perverse bow” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what this is doth better appear in that which followeth, where he saith, “And unto wrath they provoked Him with their hills” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It doth signify that they leaped into idolatry.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The bow then was perverted, not for the name of the Lord, but against the name of the Lord: who said to the same people, “Thou shalt have none other Gods but Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But by the bow He doth signify the mind’s intention.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This same idea, lastly, more clearly working out, “And in their graven idols,” he saith, “they provoked Him to indignation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God heard, and He despised:” that is, He gave heed and took vengeance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And unto nothing He brought Israel exceedingly” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when God despised, what were they who by God’s help were what they were?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But doubtless he is commemorating the doing of that thing, when they were conquered by the Philistines in the time of Heli the priest, and the Ark of the Lord was taken, and with great slaughter they were laid low.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This it is that he speaketh of.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He rejected the tabernacle of Selom, His tabernacle, where He dwelled among men” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath elegantly explained why He rejected His tabernacle, when he saith, “where He dwelled among men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore they were not worthy for Him to dwell among, why should He not reject the tabernacle, which indeed not for Himself He had established, but for their sakes, whom now He judged unworthy for Him to dwell among.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He gave over unto captivity their strength, and their beauty unto the hands of the enemy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The very Ark whereby they thought themselves invincible, and whereon they plumed themselves, he calleth their “virtue” and “beauty.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, also afterward, when they were living ill, and boasting of the temple of the Lord, He doth terrify them by a Prophet, saying, “See ye what I have done to Selom, where was My tabernacle.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He ended with the sword His people, and His inheritance He despised” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their young men the fire devoured:” that is, wrath.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And their virgins mourned not” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not even for this was there leisure, in fear of the foe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their priests fell by the sword, and their widows were not lamented” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there fell by the sword the sons of Heli, of one of whom the wife being widowed, and presently dying in child-birth, because of the same confusion could not be mourned with the distinction of a funeral.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the Lord was awakened as one sleeping” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He seemeth to sleep, when He giveth His people into the hands of those whom He hateth, when there is said to them, “Where is thy God?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He was awakened, then, like one sleeping, like a mighty man drunken with wine.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No one would dare to say this of God, save His Spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he hath spoken, as it seemeth to ungodly men reviling; as if like a drunken man He sleepeth long, when He succoureth not so speedily as men think.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He smote His enemies in the hinder parts” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 66): those, to wit, who were rejoicing that they were able to take His Ark: for they were smitten in their back-parts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which seemeth to me to be a sign of that punishment, wherewith a man will be tortured, if he shall have looked back upon things behind; which, as saith the Apostle, he ought to value as dung.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they that do so receive the Testament of God, as that they put not off from them the old vanity, are like the hostile nations, who did place the captured Ark of the Testament beside their own idols.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And yet those old things even though these be unwilling do fall: for “all flesh is hay, and the glory of man as the flower of hay.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The hay hath dried up, and the flower hath fallen off:” but the Ark of the Lord “abideth for everlasting,” to wit, the secret testament of the kingdom of Heaven, where is the eternal Word of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they that have loved things behind, because of these very things most justly shall be tormented.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “everlasting reproach He hath given to them.” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He rejected,” he saith, “the tabernacle of Joseph, and the tribe of Ephraim He chose not” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He chose the tribe of Judah” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath not said, He rejected the tabernacle of Reuben, who was the first-born son of Jacob; nor them that follow, and precede Judah in order of birth; so that they being rejected and not chosen, the tribe of Judah was chosen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it might have been said that they were deservedly rejected; because even in the blessing of Jacob wherewith he blessed his sons, he mentioneth their sins, and deeply abhorreth them; though among them the tribe of Levi merited to be the priestly tribe, whence also Moses was.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor hath he said, He rejected the tabernacle of Benjamin, or the tribe of Benjamin He chose not, out of which a king already had begun to be; for thence there had been chosen Saul; whence because of the very proximity of the time, when he had been rejected and refused, and David chosen, this might conveniently have been said; but yet was not said: but he hath named those especially who seemed to excel for more surpassing merits.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Joseph fed in Egypt his father and his brethren, and having been impiously sold, because of his piety, chastity, wisdom, he was most justly exalted; and Ephraim by the blessing of his grandfather Jacob was preferred before his elder brother: and yet God “rejected the tabernacle of Joseph, and the tribe of Ephraim He chose not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In which place by these names of renowned merit, what else do we understand but that whole people with old cupidity requiring of the Lord earthly rewards, rejected and refused, but the tribe of Judah chosen not for the sake of the merits of that same Judah?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For far greater are the merits of Joseph, but by the tribe of Judah, inasmuch as thence arose Christ according to the flesh, the Scripture doth testify of the new people of Christ preferred before that old people, the Lord opening in parables His mouth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Moreover, thence also in that which followeth, “the Mount Sion which He chose,” we do better understand the Church of Christ, not worshipping God for the sake of the carnal blessings of the present time, but from afar looking for future and eternal rewards with the eyes of faith: for Sion too is interpreted a “looking out.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly there followeth, “and He builded like as of unicorns His sanctification” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 70): or, as some interpreters have made thereof a new word, “His sanctifying.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The unicorns are rightly understood to be those, whose firm hope is uplifted unto that one thing, concerning which another Psalm saith, “One thing I have sought of the Lord, this I will require.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the sanctifying of God, according to the Apostle Peter, is understood to be a holy people and a royal priesthood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that which followeth, “in the land which He founded for everlasting:” which the Greek copies have εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα, whether it be called by us “for everlasting,” or “for an age,” is at the pleasure of the Latin translators; forasmuch as it doth signify either: and therefore the latter is found in some Latin copies, the former in others.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some also have it in the plural, that is, “for ages:” which in the Greek copies which we have had we have not found.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But which of the faithful would doubt, that the Church, even though, some going, others coming, she doth pass out of this life in mortal manner, is yet founded for everlasting?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He chose David His servant” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The tribe, I say, of Judah, for the sake of David: but David for the sake of Christ: the tribe then of Judah for the sake of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At whose passing by blind men cried out, “Have pity on us, Son of David:” and forthwith by His pity they received light, because true was the thing which they cried out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then the Apostle doth not cursorily speak of, but doth heedfully notice, writing to Timothy, “Be thou mindful, that Christ Jesus hath risen from the dead, of the seed of David,” etc. Therefore the Saviour Himself, made according to the flesh of the seed of David, is figured in this passage under the name of David, the Lord opening in parables His mouth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And let it not move us, that when he had said, “and He chose David,” under which name he signified Christ, he hath added, “His servant,” not His Son.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea even hence we may perceive, that not the substance of the Only-Begotten coeternal with the Father, but the “form of a servant” was taken of the seed of David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He took him from the flocks of sheep, from behind the teeming sheep He received him: to feed Jacob His servant, and Israel His inheritance” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This David indeed, of whose seed the flesh of Christ is, from the pastoral care of cattle was translated to the kingdom of men: but our David, Jesus Himself, from men to men, from Jews to Gentiles, was yet according to the parable from sheep to sheep taken away and translated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there are not now in that land “Churches of Judæa in Christ,” which belonged to them of the circumcision after the recent Passion and Resurrection of our Lord, of whom saith the Apostle, “But I was unknown by face to the Churches of Judæa, which are in Christ,” etc. Already from hence those Churches of the circumcised people have passed away: and thus in Judæa, which now doth exist on the earth, there is not now Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath been removed thence, now He doth feed flocks of Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly from behind teeming sheep He hath been taken thence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For those former Churches were of such sort, as that of them it is said in the Song of Songs, “Thy teeth—are like a flock of shorn ewes going up from the washing, all of which do bear twins, and a barren one is not among them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they then laid aside like as it were fleeces the burdens of the world, when before the feet of the Apostles they laid the prices of their sold goods, going up from that Laver, concerning which the apostle Peter doth admonish them, when they were troubled because they had shed the blood of Christ, and he saith, “Repent ye, and let each one of you be baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, and your sins shall be forgiven you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But twins they begat, the works, to wit, of the two commandments of twin love, love of God, and love of one’s neighbour: whence a barren one there was not among them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From behind these teeming sheep our David having been taken, doth now feed other flocks among the Gentiles, and those too “Jacob” and “Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus hath been said, “to feed Jacob His servant, and Israel His inheritance.”…Unless perchance any one be willing to make such a distinction as this; viz. that in this time Jacob serveth; but he will be the eternal inheritance of God, at that time when he shall see God face to face, whence he hath received the name Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He fed them,” he saith, “in the innocence of His heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What can be more innocent than He, who not only had not any sin whereby to be conquered, but even not any to conquer?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And in the understanding of His hands He led them home:” or, as some copies have it, “in the understandings of His hands.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Any other man might suppose that it would have been better had it been said thus, “in innocence of hands and understanding of heart;” but He who knew better than others what He spake, preferred to join with the heart innocence, and with the hands understanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is for this reason, as far as I judge; because many men think themselves innocent, who do not evil things because they fear lest they should suffer if they shall have done them; but they have the will to do them, if they could with impunity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such men may seem to have innocence of hands, but yet not that of heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what, I pray, or of what sort is that innocence, if of heart it is not, where man was made after the image of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in this which he saith, “in understanding (or intelligence) of His hands He led them home,” he seemeth to me to have spoken of that intelligence which He doth Himself make in believers: and so “of His hands:” for making doth belong to the hands, but in the sense wherein the hands of God may be understood; for even Christ was a Man in such sort, that He was also God.…
